trails.name: Murchison skyline walkway
trails.description: Murchison is centrally located in the upper South Island on State Highway 6, being 2 hours south of Nelson, 2 hours 30 minutes west of Blenheim, 1 hour 30 minutes east of Westport and 4 hours northwest of Christchurch.

trails.name: Flora Hut to Upper Takaka Junction
Salisbury Track
trails.description: Walk to Salisbury Lodge from Flora car park. This is an easy overnight tramp that's good for older children.

trails.name: Thor Creek to T.Carter suspension bridge
Cecil Kings Hut to Stone Hut
ST ARNAUD RANGE TRACK - LOWER SECTION
Ellis Basin route
trails.description: Enjoy extensive views of the Ellis Basin and the surrounding mountains on this track to Ellis Hut.

trails.name: Karamea Bend Hut to Crow River
ST ARNAUD RANGE TRACK - TOP SECTION
L MATIRI TK - WEST BRANCH TO 1000 AC JCN
Porters Hut to R. Branch Motueka River
Kiwi Track
trails.description: This old goldminer's track leads to Kiwi Saddle Hut.

trails.name: End grass flats to Cecil Kings Hut
Rolling Jctn to end of grass flats
Belltown Hut to Wangapeka Bivvy Route
Wangapeka Bivvy to Little Wanganui Sddle
MATIRI VALLEY TK (1000 AC JCN TO MCC H)
MATIRI VALLEY TK (HUR HUT TO BUSHLINE)
MATIRI VALLEY TK (MCC HUT TO HUR HUT)
1000 ACRES (1000 AC JCN TO PP HUT)
JOHNSON CREEK TRACK
trails.description: Walk to and through a big slip formed in the 1929 Murchison earthquake.

trails.name: TIRAUMEA TRACK - T.HUT TO D'U TK
TIRAUMEA TRACK - TUTAKI RD TO T.HUT
Porters Hut to Left Branch Motueka River
Red Hills Tk - Carpark to RB Motueka Riv
Baton historic bridge to Baton Saddle
Leslie/Peel confluence to Karamea Bend H
T. Carter suspension bridge to Taipo Swi
Billies/Blue Ck Jctn to Granity Pass Hut
Granity Ck to Billies Knob / Blue Ck.
Chummies Track
trails.description: Chummies Track is a steep climb up to John Reid Hut. It fords the Wangapeka River which is impassable if running high.

trails.name: Taipo Bridge to Little Wanganui Saddle
Fenian Caves Track
trails.description: Explore the Fenian Caves, with the option to visit the Fenian Workings.

trails.name: L. MATIRI TK - ROADEND TO WEST BRANCH
Flora Hut Walk
trails.description: Walk through beech forest to Flora Hut - a great day walk for families with kids.

trails.name: BARRON FLAT CAR PARK TO BUSHEDGE
Heaphy Track - Scotts Beach to Katipo
BRAEBURN WALK
trails.description: Walk to a mossy waterfall through diverse beech and podocarp forest near Lake Rotoroa.

trails.name: SKYLINE WALK
trails.description: Walk up to a viewpoint over Murchison township, and the Matakitaki, Buller and Matiri rivers.

trails.name: SIX MILE WALK
trails.description: Explore what remains of the old Six Mile hydro power station.

trails.name: SPEARGRASS TRACK
trails.description: For a good family challenge, walk to Speargrass Hut on an intermediate grade track.

trails.name: KAWATIRI HISTORIC RAILWAY WALK
trails.description: This easy walk follows a short section of the former railway line that ran from Nelson towards Murchison.

trails.name: KILLDEVIL TK-RDGTOP TO WAINGARO FK HUT
WAINGARO FORKS HUT TO YULETIDE PEAK JNCT
BULLOCK TK TO LAKE PEEL JUNCTION
Wangapeka Carpark to Belltown Hut Route
Mokihinui Rd End to Mokihinui F Hut
Lake Hanlon Track
trails.description: Walk to this scenic gem from the main road, south of Karamea.

trails.name: TC Hut to TC Suspension Bridge
Mokihinui Forks Hut to Goat Creek Hut Tk
Goat Creek Hut to Stern Hut Tk
Stern Hut to Ghost Lake Hut Tk
Ghost Lake Hut to Lyell Saddle Hut Tk
Lyell Saddle Hut to Lyell Track End
Asbestos Cottage tracks
trails.description: Walk to an historic cottage (built 1897) that was home to a reclusive couple for nearly 40 years.

trails.name: Cloustons Mine Track
trails.description: Walk this track as part of a day walk circuit from Flora car park.

trails.name: Cobb Ridge Route
trails.description: Enjoy views of Cobb Reservoir from this track that runs along the ridge above.

trails.name: Cobb valley tracks
trails.description: The Cobb valley provides options for day and overnight walks with a river and an historic hut to keep kids entertained.

trails.name: Ellis Basin Route
trails.description: Enjoy extensive views of the Ellis Basin and the surrounding mountains on this track to Ellis Hut.

trails.name: Flora Hut and Mount Arthur Hut tracks
trails.description: Walk through beech forest to one of two huts, or combine the tracks for a circuit walk - a great day walk or overnight tramp for families with kids.

trails.name: Lake Peel tracks
trails.description: Walk to the beautiful basin that holds Lake Peel.

trails.name: Lake Rotoiti Circuit
trails.description: Walk around Lake Rotoiti, surrounded by high mountain peaks. It's a great option for families with children and there is the option to catch a water taxi one way or to stay overnight in a hut. This track is part of Te Araroa Trail.

trails.name: Lake Rotoroa walking tracks
trails.description: Waterfalls, lakeside picnics and the tree fuschia await on the walking tracks of Lake Rotoroa in Nelson Lakes National Park.

trails.name: Lodestone summit
trails.description: Climb to the top of Lodestone (1462 m) and enjoy spectacular views of Tasman Bay and the Arthur Range.

trails.name: Mount Arthur summit
trails.description: Climb to the top of Mt Arthur (1795 m) for panoramic views.

trails.name: Mount Robert Circuit
trails.description:
This loop track around the northern face of Pourangahau/Mount
Robert offers spectacular views over Lake Rotoiti that will keep even kids entertained. It can be enjoyed as a day or overnight walk.

trails.name: Mt Owen tracks and routes
trails.description: At 1875 m Mt Owen is the highest peak in Kahurangi National Park and on a fine day the views from this peak are spectacular.

trails.name: St Arnaud Range Track
trails.description: Climb onto the St Arnaud Range for spectacular views of the surrounding area.

trails.name: Sylvester Hut Track
trails.description: Walk up to Sylvester Hut as day walk or overnight tramp with children - enjoy great views from the hut and explore a series of lakes on the open tops behind the hut.

trails.name: Whisky Falls
trails.description: Walk to these 40 m falls surrounded by thick walls of moss and ferns.

trails.name: Lake Rotoiti (West Bay) walks
trails.description: Explore West Bay on Lake Rotoiti on these short walks.

trails.name: Lake Rotoiti (Kerr Bay) walks
trails.description: Explore Kerr Bay on Lake Rotoiti on these short walks and experience the outcomes of the Rotoiti Nature Recovery Project.

trails.name: Ōpārara Valley Track
trails.description: Extend the Moria Gate Loop short walk and experience ancient native forest on a longer walk.

trails.name: Ōpārara Basin: Moria Gate and Mirror Tarn Loop
trails.description: Enjoy a beautiful limestone arch and spectacular rainforest reflections on a short walk that kids will love.

trails.name: Ōpārara Arch
trails.description: Walk to a magnificent limestone arch - it's easy, interesting and great for kids.

trails.name: Fenian Caves Track
trails.description: Explore the Fenian Caves, with the option to visit the Fenian Workings.

trails.name: Big Rimu Walk
trails.description: Walk to a spectacular rimu tree, 36 m high and 2 m in diameter, near Karamea.

trails.name: Kōhaihai area short walks
trails.description: Enjoy beautiful nīkau forest on these easy walks from Kōhaihai Campsite.

trails.name: Adams Flat Track
trails.description: Walk to a replica goldminer's hut and the site of the Adams Creek Workings.

trails.name: Lake Matiri Hut Track
trails.description: Walk to Lake Matiri Hut on the shores of Lake Matiri.

trails.name: Angelus Hut via Travers and Cascade Track/Route
trails.description: Continue on from Lakehead or Coldwater Hut to Cascade Track, and on to Angelus Hut following the  Hukere Stream.

trails.name: Angelus Hut via Mt Cedric Track/Route
trails.description: Continue from Sabine Hut to Angelus Hut on a very steep route.

trails.name: Angelus Hut via Pinchgut Track and Robert Ridge Route
trails.description: The most popular route to Angelus Hut in fine weather.

trails.name: Angelus Hut via Speargrass Track and Speargrass Creek Route
trails.description: This route is often used to exit Rotomaninitua/Lake Angelus in bad weather. There are unbridged stream crossings, and muddy sections.
